source.idea: A train dispatcher hears a distress call from a train removed from the timetable thirty years ago.

B.narration:
In the heart of the night, Jack Thompson sat alone in the dispatch center, the glow of monitors illuminating his tired face.
Another quiet night... just me and the trains, I guess.
Suddenly, a crackling noise broke the silence, drawing Jack's attention to the radio.
What the...? This frequency hasn't been used in years!
Jack leaned closer, heart racing as he heard a faint voice calling for help, echoing through the static.
This can't be real... it's from the old ghost train!
The ghost train, a legend whispered among dispatchers, had vanished thirty years ago, leaving behind only rumors.
I need to find out what's happening...
Jack frantically typed on his keyboard, searching for any record of the train's last known location.
Come on, show me something!
The screen flickered, revealing a hidden file dated back to the night the train disappeared, sending chills down Jack's spine.
This can't be... it's like it was never erased!
As he opened the file, a map appeared, pinpointing a location deep in the woods, long abandoned and forgotten.
I have to go there... I have to know!
Jack grabbed his jacket and flashlight, determination flooding his veins as he stepped out into the night.
This could be the biggest discovery of my career... or the last thing I ever do.
The woods were dense and eerie, the moonlight barely piercing through the thick canopy as Jack made his way to the coordinates.
Just a little further... I can do this!
Suddenly, the sound of a train whistle echoed through the trees, sending a jolt of fear through Jack's body.
No way... it can't be!
He stumbled upon the old tracks, rusted and overgrown, but unmistakably leading to a ghostly silhouette in the distance.
There it is... the ghost train!
As he approached, the train's doors creaked open, revealing darkness inside, a chilling invitation.
I have to go in... I have to find out!
Jack stepped inside, the air thick with dust and memories, as the doors slammed shut behind him, trapping him in darkness.
What have I done?
Suddenly, ghostly figures appeared, their faces twisted in anguish, revealing the train's tragic past.
No... this can't be real!
Jack realized he was not just a witness; he was now part of the haunting, trapped in the train's eternal journey.
I need to escape!
As the train began to move, Jack's screams echoed into the night, swallowed by the darkness of the woods.
Help! Someone, please!

characters.name: Jack Thompson

settings.name: Train Dispatch Center

A.narration:
The hum of the station was a familiar tune to Martin, consistent like the tide. He sat in his glass-walled booth, eyes scanning the array of blinking lights on the control panel. A day like any other, until a crackling voice shattered the routine.
Central Dispatch here. Identify yourself, over.
This is Train 713. We require urgent assistance, over.
Martin's heart skipped. Train 713 was an echo from the past, a phantom from thirty years ago. He leaned in, gripping the microphone tighter.
Train 713, this is Central Dispatch. Repeat your last, over.
Silence stretched, as if the radio itself held its breath. Then the voice returned, clearer now, tinged with desperation.
We're lost in the fog. Passengers are panicking. Please, help us.
Martin rummaged through dusty archives, pages yellowed with age. The story of Train 713 haunted these files. A storm, a derailment, all souls lost. He flipped through the logs, a chill creeping up his spine.
This can’t be real. How can they be calling now?
The station intercom buzzed, pulling him back to the present. Another voice, but this one from today.
Martin, what are you doing with those old logs? We’ve got live trains to manage.
Martin sprinted to the platform, eyes scanning the horizon where fog clung like a shroud. He clutched the receiver, his voice a lifeline thrown into the abyss.
Train 713, I’m here. Guide me to your location.
The fog swirled, parting like a curtain to reveal the gleaming outline of a train. Ghostly, yet undeniably there. As Martin approached, the voices faded, merging with the whistle of the wind.
The platform stood empty, the vision gone. Martin lingered, the echo of that desperate call still whispering in his mind. But on the control panel, a single light blinked, a reminder that even ghosts leave traces.
I won’t forget you. None of you.
The station returned to its familiar rhythm, yet Martin knew it would never quite sound the same again.

plan.topic: A haunting call from the past
